h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the centre mark has to come first.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to keep struggling with the label step and defaulting to the rule, as in L1 and L2.

scored.recoheredEarlier: part of me put it there because the claim felt risky after the seminar comment
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were converging on a simple editorial fix — remove or replace 'may' to satisfy the rubric's demand for an assertive claim.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: "Identified stops" was overclaiming; Pine should stay in next inquiry until there is evidence.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is steadily refining the problem sentence toward greater specificity, so the next step would be to name a concrete criterion for 'focused.'

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, that is sharper: "against" is not the same as "confirmed by."
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward accepting the historian's paragraph as the stronger source once it could be shown to name multiple records, with the diary demoted to single-witness status.
The learner is expected to continue working step-by-step through the dichotomous key, naming each feature in sequence.
The learner consistently applies the checklist-versus-patient-state distinction across all turns, so each successive turn continues exactly where the prior turns were heading.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it, that docket is like the storm sneaking into the price tag
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue demanding documentary proof that the price rise is not padded, consistent with the skeptical-but-open stance established from the start.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The edge doubled. Not the count.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The earlier claim is sitting there looking rather wrong now.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a straightforward path of incremental correction, expected to simply restate the right numbers in a tidier form.
